scheme_name: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Old and Infirm
page_title: Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa - Old and Infirm

details: The scheme “Mukhyamantri Samajik Sahayata Prakalpa- Old and Infirm" was launched by the Directorate of Social Welfare and Social Education, Government of Tripura. This scheme aims to provide financial assistance to poor people of Tripura who are old and infirm, such as senior citizens who are 60 years and above in age.
benefits: The pension amount of ₹2000/- per month will be transferred directly to the bank account of the applicant.
The list of beneficiaries will be made available on the
of the Social Welfare & Social Education Department, Govt. of Tripura.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Tripura.
The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹1,00,000/-
The age of the applicant should be 60 years or above.
If the family has children in the age group of 6 to 14 years, those children must be enrolled in a school recognized by the Government of Tripura.
The applicant should be affected by Cancer/ AIDS/ Leprosy.
The assistance under this scheme will be stopped immediately in the following cases:
2. Fulfillment of any exclusion criteria during the lifetime of a beneficiary:
A member of the family of the beneficiary getting a Government Job.
The annual family income of the beneficiary exceeds ₹1,00,000/-.

offline: The applicant should visit the Office of the Child Development Project Officer and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the
The interested applicant should take print of the
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the CDPO Office under which the project his/her residence is located.

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

documents_required: Income Certificate from SDM (Sub Divisional Magistrate).
Age Proof Certificate (Birth Certificate/ School Certificate/ Madhyamik Admit).
Ration Card, issued by the Food & Civil Supplies Department.
Permanent Resident of Tripura Certificate (PRTC), issued by the Sub Divisional Administration.
Non-Government Employee Certificate from Panchayat Secretary/Ward Secretary/Gazetted Officer.
Life Certificate & Income Certificate are to be submitted to the CDPO in November every year.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of the scheme is to provide substantial financial benefits to poor people in Tripura who are old or infirm.

faqs.question: What events lead to the immediate cessation of assistance under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Assistance is stopped in cases such as the death of the beneficiary or fulfillment of exclusion criteria, including remarriage of a widow or a family member getting a Government Job.

faqs.question: What is the condition related to the enrollment of children in the family?
faqs.answer: If the family has children aged 6 to 14 years, they must be enrolled in a school recognized by the Government of Tripura

faqs.question: What is the maximum annual income limit for the family of the applicant?
faqs.answer: The annual income of the applicant's family should not exceed ₹1,00,000.

faqs.question: Where can the beneficiaries check the list of beneficiaries for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The list of beneficiaries is available on the official website of the Social Welfare & Social Education Department, Govt. of Tripura.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria regarding residency for applicants?
faqs.answer: Applicants must be residents of Tripura to qualify for the scheme.

faqs.question: How much is the monthly pension amount provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The pension amount under the scheme is ₹2,000 per month.

faqs.question: What is the mode of disbursal for the sanctioned grant?
faqs.answer: The sanctioned grant is directly transferred to the individual bank account of the applicant.

faqs.question: How often should the Life Certificate and Income Certificate be submitted after selection?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries need to submit the Life Certificate & Income Certificate to the CDPO in November every year after selection.

faqs.question: Who Grants the Final Approval for Sanctioning Assistance under the Scheme?
faqs.answer: The final approval for sanctioning assistance under the scheme is granted by the Hon'ble Minister of the Department of Social Welfare & Social Education, Government of Tripura."

faqs.question: When will the money be taken back from the beneficiary or their nominee's account?
faqs.answer: The money will be taken back from the beneficiary or their nominee's account due to delayed reporting of death of the beneficiary and family member's get government job.
